name: Nipsey Russell
abstract: Julius "Nipsey" Russell (September 15, 1918 – October 2, 2005) was an American entertainer best known for his appearances as a panelist on game shows from the 1960s through the 1990s, including Match Game, Password, Hollywood Squares, To Tell the Truth, and Pyramid. His appearances were often distinguished by short, humorous poems he rec…

concerts.label: Tribute to Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr.
Midnight Performance: New York Jazz Festival

about_quotes.speaker: Ruth Brisbane
about_quotes.evidence: when my sister-in-law called me for the Baby Grand in Brooklyn and Nipsey Russell was the emcee... I was afraid for him to even say boo to me like “hello” — this is Nipsey Russell.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ruth Brisbane interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 2, 2013

about_quotes.speaker: Lou Donaldson
about_quotes.evidence: By that time, Nipsey had moved to New York, and he brought me around to the Baby Grand. I got to meet all the musicians.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lou Donaldson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
